claim: All players in a Seamless Coop session must use the same mods to connect; visual mods are the documented exception and may be kept personal.
quote: For optimal experience, all players should use the same mods together - with the exception for visual mods, which can remain personal.

claim: Seamless Coop must be launched via the bundled ersc_launcher.exe or via ModEngine 2 — DLL injectors such as Elden Mod Loader and Lazy Loader are explicitly unsupported.
quote: You MUST launch Seamless Coop with either ersc_launcher.exe or ModEngine2. DLL injectors like Elden Mod Loader and Lazy Loader won't work.

claim: Mods must match the current Elden Ring version; abandoned/outdated mods will not work and only the latest game version is typically supported.
quote: Mods have to be compatible with the version of Elden Ring you're using. Typically only the latest version of Elden Ring is supported, and if the mod has been abandoned you will not be able to use it.

claim: Everyone in a session must be running the same regulation.bin (param) mods; the FAQ enumerates this as a required check when a session fails.
quote: NOTE: Make sure everyone is using the same regulation.bin file mods.

claim: Small regulation.bin changes from FromSoftware patches won't break Seamless Coop, but every player must run identical game and mod versions to connect.
quote: Small changes (like changes to regulation.bin) will not break Seamless Coop. However, everyone has to be on the same game and mod version in order to connect to each other.

claim: ModEngine 2 has no built-in conflict resolution for regulation.bin — the highest-priority mod's regulation.bin wins, which is why all players must agree on it for Seamless Coop to match.
quote: Note that modengine 2 currently has no way to resolve conflicting files including regulation.bin, and thus the mod with the highest priority will have the modded file be loaded in the case of conflict.

claim: As of v1.9.5, Seamless Coop unified its matchmaking with ModEngine 2/3 users — provided they share an identical regulation file — confirming regulation.bin parity is the canonical desync/matchmaking gate.
quote: ME2 and ME3 users are no longer in isolated matchmaking pools (providing the regulation file is consistent)

claim: Host's ersc_settings.ini is authoritative for the world: scaling, invasions, rot debuffs, and spirit summons are all gated by host settings.
quote: Host's ersc_settings.ini determines the world's Scaling, Player Invasions, Rot and Spirit Summons.

claim: The session password lives in seamlesscoopsettings.ini / ersc_settings.ini and must be identical across all players; mismatched passwords prevent connection.
quote: Make sure everyone has set the same password in the seamlesscoopsettings.ini. You must save the file and restart the game entirely for the new password to be registered.

claim: The save_file_extension setting (default 'co2') lets players keep separate save banks per mod combination so vanilla-Seamless saves don't get clobbered by modded sessions.
quote: Your save file extension (in the vanilla game this is .sl2). Use any alphanumeric characters

claim: The allow_invaders host toggle in [GAMEPLAY] enables/disables PvP invaders entering the host world — a host-side gate.
quote: Invaders are other players that will join your world uninvited and try to kill you and your party. 0=FALSE 1=TRUE allow_invaders = 1

claim: Players without the DLC can still co-op the base game, but if a non-owner enters DLC space the result is character corruption and infinite loading screens.
quote: Yes, but you will only be able to play the base game together. Attempts to enter the DLC despite that can result in destroyed characters and infinite loading screens.

claim: Anti-cheat software like Riot Vanguard is flagged by the official FAQ as a known cause of Seamless Coop session-join failure.
quote: If you have installed Riot's Vanguard installed try turning it off. If you have any other games anti cheat that is known to be intrusive turn them off as well.

claim: Shadow of the Erdtree compatibility was added by a major Seamless Coop rewrite (v1.8.0, 2024-07-19) within ~3 weeks of DLC release.
quote: Elden Ring's Popular Seamless Co-Op Mod Playable Once More After Creator Re-Writes It to Work With Shadow of the Erdtree

claim: Seamless Co-op by Yui (mod 510) is the foundation mod, latest update integrates SotE
quote: Compatibility with game version 1.14

claim: Era's/Transmogrify Armor (mod 3596) author rewrote networking to make transmogs sync in Seamless Co-op; explicitly DLC compatible.
quote: Change your appearance to any armor while keeping the stats and bonuses from the armor you actually have equipped

claim: Armor Dyes (mod 6927) by Tom Clark is explicitly Seamless Co-op compatible; dyes sync to other players if they also have the mod.
quote: Compatible with Seamless Co-op, The Convergence, Reforged, Transmog

claim: Photorealistic Lands Between ReShade (mod 45) — community confirmed working under Seamless Co-op.
quote: Photorealistic & Vivid Lands Between ReShade Collection

claim: REVENANT ReShade (mod 8) — long-running ReShade preset bundle (LUSH, GRIM); ReShade is post-process only, safe with Seamless Co-op.
quote: Install the latest ReShade(currently 5.0.2) then select REVENANT from the preset dropdown

claim: Skip the intro logos (mod 421) — client-side launch flow tweak, doesn't touch regulation/save.
quote: Skips the intro logos and covers up the initial blinding white screen when you start the game.

claim: Adjust the field of view (mod 325) — client-side FoV slider, no regulation.bin or save data changes.
quote: Edit the FoV value inside mods\AdjustTheFov\config.ini

claim: Texture Improvement (mod 2431) by Bardo — pure texture replacer; ERSC docs say only local player sees them.
quote: A texture overhaul for Elden Ring

claim: Verdigris armor and shield 4K (mod 5753) — pure texture replacement, post-SotE upload.
quote: Verdigris armor and shield - 4K

claim: EZ Auto Backup explicitly Seamless Co-Op compatible per its title; provides Seamless save bank profile.
quote: EZ Auto Backup (Seamless Co-Op compatible)

claim: Seamless Co-op Custom Armor Fixer (mod 3818) — companion that fixes the issue where custom armor doesn't render on other players' clients.
quote: Fixes the issue where custom armor cannot be seen on other players

claim: ERSC official modding doc states only the player sees their own texture/model mods.
quote: NOTE2: Only you will see the texture and modle modifications you are using.

claim: Custom armor / parts mods are invisible to other players unless both the high-LOD and a duplicated low-LOD '_l' suffixed copy exist.
quote: this is why custom armor does not appear on your friends

claim: Both players must run the LOD-fixer batch file AND have the same armor mod files installed for cosmetic armor to appear in co-op.
quote: It also goes without saying every player must also have the same Armor mod/s

claim: Seamless 1.9.5 fixed isolated matchmaking pools for ME2/ME3 users.
quote: ME2 and ME3 users are no longer in isolated matchmaking pools (providing the regulation file is consistent)

claim: Seamless 1.8.8 explicitly fixed an issue where alternative launchers severely restricted matchmaking AND scaled back anticheat to allow QoL/performance mods.
quote: Scaled back some anticheat measures for invasion matchmaking to allow for some QoL and performance improvement mods.

claim: ModEngine2 + Seamless Co-op load order: SeamlessCoop's ersc.dll must be added under external_dlls in config_eldenring.toml.
quote: in 'config_eldenring.toml' file, 'SeamlessCoop/ersc.dll' line added under the 'external_dlls = [' parameter

claim: Every Elden Ring patch from FromSoft breaks Seamless Co-op until LukeYui pushes an update; the launcher silently falls back to vanilla offline mode.
quote: every Elden Ring update (by FromSoftware) is expected to break all the mods, including Seamless Coop, until they're updated

claim: Shadow of the Erdtree broke Seamless Co-op in the immediate post-DLC window; players had to wait for LukeYui or roll back the game version.
quote: The DLC was not compatible with the mod in the days after its release, or with the subsequent updates.

claim: Host-side ersc_settings.ini directly controls cooppassword (must match across all players), allow_invaders, scaling, save file naming. Guest's ini is ignored for these.
quote: To join a game, players need to have the same co-op password set in the seamlesscoopsettings.ini file on their PC.

claim: Seamless Experimental 2.0 is explicitly NOT mod-compatible.
quote: Not compatible with mods; The seamless co-op launcher is required for functionality. Wait for stable release if you require usage of other mods.

claim: Snuggly's Cinematic ReShade is a popular ReShade preset, post-process only.
quote: Must have ReShade installed for this to work

claim: Natural Realism ReShade is a popular preset, RTGI-compatible.
quote: ReShade preset that aims to give the game's visuals a beautiful and simple upgrade while staying true to the native color palette

claim: Bardo's HD Texture Pack is a 25GB AI-upscaled environmental texture overhaul; armors and characters untouched.
quote: All armors and characters are untouched

claim: Texture Improvement v4 installs via ModEngine2 mod folder.
quote: Mod Engine 2: Place the contents of the zip files inside your modengine/mod folder.

claim: Enhanced 4K facial textures is recent (2026-01) AI-upscaled facial texture mod, flagged Safe to use.
quote: This mod is an upscaled version of the facial textures to 4k resolution.

claim: FromSoft uses high/low LOD parts system: other players render the low '_l' variant from THEIR own files, so player-character mods are invisible to mod-less peers by default.
quote: The player is naturally the high version with low reserved for other online invaders, co-operators

claim: AdjustTheFov by techiew uses Elden Mod Loader DLL plugin — no regulation.bin modification.
quote: You only need Elden Mod Loader, which automatically loads the mod on startup.

claim: Seamless Co-op v1.9.0 is the current stable release with 70k+ endorsements.
quote: Seamless Co-op (Elden Ring)

claim: Visual-only mods can remain personal per-player; gameplay/file mods (movesets, regulation.bin) require all players to use the same mods.
quote: For optimal experience, all players should use the same mods together - with the exception for visual mods, which can remain personal.

claim: Moveset Animation Remix v2.4 is built specifically for Vanilla+ Seamless Co-op play but explicitly fixes hitboxes and TAE data.
quote: lots of bugfixes of stuff that not working anymore with DLC update

claim: ERSC documentation classifies moveset packs (Clever's) as gameplay-affecting overhauls bundled with regulation.bin edits.
quote: mods are usually overhauls like Clever's moveset packs, Convergence and Elden Ring Reforged

claim: Transmogrify Armor v2.4.0 was rewritten to use Steamworks SDK so transmogs reliably propagate via Seamless Co-op networking.
quote: Rewrite multiplayer code to use the Steamworks SDK for networking, which should make transmogs reliably show up in Seamless Co-op

claim: Transmogrify Armor explicitly does not block matchmaking; players without it can still co-op or invade.
quote: This mod doesn't impact Seamless Co-op matchmaking, so you can still co-op or invade with people who don't have it installed

claim: Transmogrify Armor ships a client_side_only INI flag that prevents the transmog from being seen online.
quote: Add client_side_only option to prevent transmogs from being seen online

claim: Transmogrify Armor is dangerous for vanilla online (non-Seamless) play; only safe for offline or Seamless Co-op.
quote: Playing online with transmogrified armor won't work, and might get you banned if you try anyways.

claim: Bloodborne UI Remastered is a pure asset/texture replacement of UI elements and icons.
quote: Recreation of ER's UI using Bloodborne assets.

claim: Closed Network HUD recreates the PS4 Network Test HUD via menu/HUD asset swaps.
quote: Recreated Closed Network HUD for PC version

claim: Fully Toggle HUD UI is a Reshade ShaderToggler add-on; explicitly cleared by author for Seamless Co-op.
quote: Do not use this in online mode! Reshade with add-on support might get you banned. You have to uninstall Reshade, if you want to play online. (Unless it's seamless co-op)

claim: Seamless Co-op explicitly forbids DLL injectors other than ersc_launcher.exe and ModEngine 2.
quote: DLL injectors like Elden Mod Loader and Lazy Loader won't work.
